name: Icelandic Magic
description: Icelandic magic, also known as Galdr or Seiðr, is a complex system of magical practices rooted in Norse paganism and Icelandic folklore. It involves the manipulation of energies, the use of runes, spells, and charms to influence events and individuals. Originating in pre-Christian Scandinavia, it was brought to Iceland by settlers and evolved over centuries, incorporating local beliefs and influences. Icelandic magic encompasses various techniques, including divination, healing, protection, and cursing. It often involved ritualistic practices, chanting, and the creation of magical objects like staves and sigils. The practice was deeply intertwined with daily life, used for everything from ensuring a good harvest to warding off evil spirits. While officially suppressed with the adoption of Christianity, it persisted in folk traditions and has experienced a revival in modern times. Its cultural significance lies in its connection to Iceland's unique history, its relationship with nature, and its enduring power to shape beliefs and practices.
subtitle: Galdr and Seiðr of the North

keyMyths.title: The Creation of Runes
keyMyths.description: Odin hung himself on Yggdrasil for nine days and nights to gain the wisdom of the runes. He then shared this knowledge with humanity, granting them access to magical power.

keyMyths.title: The Tale of Þórgunna
keyMyths.description: Þórgunna was a powerful völva known for her skills in prophecy and healing. Her story highlights the respected, yet feared, position of women in Icelandic magical practices.

sources.author: Stephen Flowers
sources.work: Icelandic Magic: Practical Secrets of the Northern Grimoires
sources.description: A modern exploration of Icelandic grimoires and magical practices.
sources.text: A modern exploration of Icelandic grimoires and magical practices.
